activity_title: Set default theme to Light Mode and hide redundant All-PH entry on single-PH dashboards
activity_summary: Set the master dashboard default theme to Light Mode at the root (html data-theme=light, initTheme fallback light, static toggle label Light). Added a renderPhRail guard so the All Portfolio Holders sidebar entry renders only when more than one PH exists; on a single-PH dashboard the lone holder renders selected. Regenerated all 24 Portfolio Holder dashboards from the updated master so they inherit both changes. JavaScript identical across master and PH files; only dashboardData differs.
evidence_refs: master default light verified; 24 PH dashboards regenerated; 0 shell drift vs master
reusable_pattern: Single master shell plus per-entity data block; guard UI affordances by row count so one JS serves master and single-entity views
validation_rule: PH dashboards must be byte-identical to master outside the data markers; single-PH view hides the All entry
gap_or_risk: Single-PH dashboards previously showed a redundant All Portfolio Holders entry and defaulted to dark

activity_title: Resolved CSS browser-compatibility warnings for deployment
activity_summary: Fixed Microsoft Edge Tools compat-api/css warnings flagged for deployment. Replaced color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) X percent, transparent) with the exact rgba(79,156,249,.X) equivalent (accent is always blue, identical in light and dark) in 5 places; replaced the Firefox-only scrollbar-width and scrollbar-color with widely-supported ::-webkit-scrollbar rules. Applied to the master, index.html, and all 24 PH dashboards. Zero flagged properties remain across all 26 HTML files.
evidence_refs: 0 color-mix / scrollbar-width / scrollbar-color occurrences remaining across 26 files; visuals unchanged
reusable_pattern: For deploy-safe CSS, replace color-mix on a fixed variable with the literal rgba; replace scrollbar-width with ::-webkit-scrollbar for Chromium/Edge/Safari
validation_rule: No color-mix(), scrollbar-width, or scrollbar-color in any deployed dashboard HTML
gap_or_risk: color-mix unsupported in Chrome below 111; scrollbar-width unsupported in Chrome below 121 / Safari / Samsung

activity_title: Integrated per-PH dashboard generation into the automatic refresh
activity_summary: Extended refresh_dashboard.py so every scheduled refresh, after building and validating the master leakage_dashboard.html, reuses the SAME in-memory dashboardData to regenerate one standalone dashboard per Portfolio Holder. Added build_ph_data (filters summary, account_summary, ph_summary, verification_summary, l1-l5 to one PH) and generate_ph_dashboards (copies the master shell, replaces only the marker block, clears stale files). No extra PostgreSQL queries, no duplicated business logic or rendering. Added per-PH validation (shell byte-identical plus PH-isolated) and total-time logging.
evidence_refs: unit test 24 PH files in 0.024s, 0 shell drift, 0 isolation failures; no SQL in PH step
reusable_pattern: Generate dashboardData once from one query set, then fan out per-entity files by swapping only the data marker block; verify each by shell-equality plus isolation
validation_rule: PH generation must issue zero SQL and keep every PH file byte-identical to master outside the data markers
gap_or_risk: Per-PH dashboards were static snapshots not part of the automated refresh
next_action: Schedule the Monday cron run end to end

activity_title: End-to-end automatic refresh PASS with Monday-to-Sunday window verified
activity_summary: Ran the full refresh_dashboard.py end to end: PostgreSQL one query set to master dashboardData to leakage_dashboard.html to 24 PH dashboards to validation to PASS, in 477.4s total (PH generation 0.02s). Verified the reporting window auto-resolved to the previous complete week Monday 2026-06-22 to Sunday 2026-06-28 (report_date 2026-06-28) even though run on a Tuesday. Counts L1 147 / L2 270 / L3 45 / L4 40 / L5 10; L1 equals COUNT(DISTINCT ASIN); no UNATTRIBUTED in ph_summary; 24 PHs, both accounts.
reusable_pattern: Anchor weekly windows to date_trunc week CURRENT_DATE so an off-Monday run still reports the previous complete Mon-Sun week
validation_rule: Refresh PASS requires markers, valid JSON, L1=distinct ASIN, no contamination, no UNATTRIBUTED, and a previous Mon-Sun window
gap_or_risk: Counts are time-dependent live values and will differ from older snapshots

activity_title: Diagnosed MCP large-HTML upload limit and prepared Team Leader upload strategy
activity_summary: Investigated why the 232 KB html_content cannot be uploaded from this environment. Found three walls: the DB host pg.severdigitweb.uk is NXDOMAIN on the system resolver and public DNS (8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1) so psql and psycopg2 cannot connect; the MCP execute_sql channel is SQL-text only and cannot read a local file; and the harness saves or strips large tool output so the bytes cannot transit through context for chunked SQL. Concluded byte-exact upload is not feasible in-environment and prepared a reliable psycopg2 one-liner for the TL or a connected host plus post-upload md5 and byte verification.
evidence_refs: NXDOMAIN on 8.8.8.8 and 1.1.1.1; outbound to 8.8.8.8:53 OK; html 237665 bytes md5 6d689e06
reusable_pattern: When MCP cannot transit large content, load it from a host that resolves the DB by reading the file into a bound parameter; verify by md5 and octet_length
validation_rule: Stored html_content must match the source file by byte count and md5 or sha256 before reporting PASS
gap_or_risk: No direct DB route from the build box; large artifacts need an external loader
next_action: TL runs the psycopg2 loader and confirms md5 6d689e06 and 237665 bytes

activity_title: Prepared validated HTML handoff and upload/validation prompts
activity_summary: Per the revised upload workflow, validated and read leakage_dashboard.html as plain text only (no encode, chunk, SQL, or DB write) and reported path, 237665 bytes, sha256 d07f8d54, and validation PASS for the TL handoff. Authored the upload and validation prompts and reporting (row preview, SQL preview, risk check, PASS or FAIL) and the post-load read-back verification steps for ph_task.
evidence_refs: sha256 d07f8d54; 237665 bytes; validation ALL PASS; no DB write in prep step
reusable_pattern: Separate validate-and-prepare from upload; hand off the artifact with hash and size so the uploader can verify exactness
validation_rule: Read-only prep must not execute SQL or modify the artifact; report hash and size
